subject: Statistics
title: The Log-Normal Distribution
description: A short lesson to help you apply The Log-Normal Distribution.

hook.text: Stock prices, city sizes, and file sizes aren't normal. But take their logarithm — a bell curve appears.

buildup.text: A log-normal distribution is normal on a log scale. Take the log and the skew vanishes.

discovery.text: Multiplicative growth creates this shape. Salary raises compounding over years produce log-normal incomes.

twist.text: Many things we call 'normal' are actually log-normal. The confusion leads to wrong models constantly.

climax.text: Most 'big number' phenomena in nature and economics follow log-normal, not normal, patterns.

punchline.text: Multiply enough small effects and the world gets skewed.

quiz.question: What creates a log-normal distribution?
options: Multiplicative growth — many small factors multiplying together
Subtracting the mean from each data point
